rationale: The US-Iran exchange of strikes continues for a second day, but this is a continuation of the known background situation with no qualitative escalation — Hormuz closure and energy price risk for Poland persists at a moderate level, while all other headlines are either irrelevant to the user or deep background.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue for second day; Hormuz closure confirmed
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day, and Al Jazeera confirms Iran has shut the Strait of Hormuz. This is a continuation of the known conflict, but the confirmed Hormuz closure sustains indirect energy price risk for Poland — no qualitative escalation beyond what was already known.

top_events.title: Trump: 'I love the inflation' as US prices hit 3-year high
top_events.summary: US inflation continues rising at its fastest rate in three years, with Trump expressing indifference. This is a continuation of the known situation — indirect risk to Polish economy via global financial conditions and potential Fed policy shifts, but no new shock this cycle.

top_events.title: Powerful earthquake kills 37 in southern Philippines
top_events.summary: A significant earthquake struck the southern Philippines, killing at least 37 people. Geographically distant from Poland with no meaningful economic or security connection — minimal impact on the user's daily life.
